mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS levels over a 3-year rolling window with Rank IC > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.5%, labor market softening triggers recession fears that immediately depress equity valuations within the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals broad-based consumer demand strength, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon through earnings growth expectations.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.05 in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days during expansion phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during confirmed expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher total nonfarm payrolls indicate robust employment growth that sustains consumer spending and corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS levels show zero or negative correlation with 20-day forward SPX returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index exceeds 198.0, persistent inflation expectations force markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in > 70% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 198.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent upward trends in quarterly GDP growth and monthly retail sales confirm broad-based demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days through sustained earnings growth.
failure_condition: Co-movement of GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with a widening unemployment rate signals a policy-induced economic slowdown, which suppresses corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance (p > 0.05) during tightening regimes.
